nodes.text: Oh — did you scan my card? Is something the matter? I've never had a problem in twenty-two years.

responses.text: Just routine verification. Everything looks fine so far.
I need to confirm a couple of details with you.
I want to ask about your prescription.

nodes.text: Oh, of course. I'm sorry — I always get a little nervous when someone scans things. Force of habit from the library, I think. We were very careful about patron records.

responses.text: Nothing to worry about. Your records look good.
Can you confirm your date of birth for me?

nodes.text: Of course. Name, date of birth, address — I have it all memorized at this point. Twenty-two years of the same forms.

responses.text: Can you confirm your date of birth?
Can you confirm your current address?

nodes.text: The amlodipine? Five milligrams, thirty tablets, Dr. Ellison — same as always. She's very consistent. Is there a problem with the prescription?

responses.text: Just verifying. Your history here is excellent.

nodes.text: Oh, I'll save you the trouble — March fourteenth, nineteen fifty-seven. I always remember it because it's Pi Day. My students used to make a big fuss about it.
Same house I've been in for forty-one years. Four twelve Birchwood Lane. I keep meaning to update my driver's license photo — it's from 1989.

responses.text: Address checks out. You're all set.

nodes.text: Wonderful. Thank you for being thorough. Mr. Hargrove always said a careful pharmacist was worth more than a fast one.
Twenty-two years and never a hiccup. I appreciate the care you take.
